mnemonics: HERE Comes The Real Data! (Hierarchical, Entity-Relationship, Conceptual, Relational, Dimensional)
Relational models are like tables in a spreadsheet, Hierarchical models are like a family tree, and Network models are like a web of interconnected nodes.
keyPoints: A data model is a collection of concepts for describing data, data relationships, data semantics, and consistency constraints.
The main types of data models are Hierarchical, Network, Relational, Entity-Relationship, Object-Based, and Dimensional.
The Relational Model is the most widely used model, organizing data into tables (relations) with rows (tuples) and columns (attributes).
commonMistakes: Confusing the Entity-Relationship Model (a conceptual, high-level model) with the Relational Model (an implementation model).
Assuming the Hierarchical and Network models are obsolete; they are still used in specific legacy systems like IMS.
